Moses Begins His Song

Deuteronomy 32

Select a column header to see its column.

The Song of Moses


(Revelation 15:1–4)

NoteCited in Philippians 2:15
NoteDSS; LXX according to the number of the angels of God; MT according to the number of the sons of Israel
NoteLiterally the pupil
NotePinions are the outer parts of a bird’s wings, including the flight feathers.
NoteJeshurun means the upright one, a term of endearment for Israel.
NoteOr not a nation; see also LXX.
NoteCited in Romans 10:19
NoteHebrew; LXX but our enemies are void of understanding
NoteLXX; Hebrew Vengeance is Mine, and recompense; cited in Romans 12:19 and Hebrews 10:30
NoteOr will judge His people; see also LXX; cited in Hebrews 10:30
NoteSee DSS and LXX; MT lacks Rejoice, O heavens, with Him, and let all God’s angels worship Him. Cited in Hebrews 1:6
NoteCited in Romans 15:10
NoteDSS and LXX; MT servants
NoteLXX and Vulgate; MT lacks and repay those who hate Him
NoteLXX, Syriac, and Vulgate; Hebrew Hoshea, a variant of Joshua

Moses’ Death Foretold
